Service

PDF to 3D Model Conversion

Send a PDF drawing. Get back a Revit model. Coordinated, parameterised, and on your house template.

Converts 2D PDF drawings (and DWGs) into a fully-modelled, parameterised Revit model — built on your office template, with your families, ready for issue.

Convert a PDF → See related apps
Hoursnot days for a complete Revit model
House templatehonoured exactly
House familiesswapped in automatically
Ambiguity-awareasks before guessing

PDF to Revit model conversion for MEP engineers

Send a 2D PDF (or a DWG, RVT, or IFC) into the conversion pipeline. The agent identifies systems by line-style, layer, and symbol, models them in 3D using your house Revit template and your house families, populates the relevant parameters, and produces a coordinated Revit model.

Where the source drawing is ambiguous (annotation overlap, unclear connections, missing system labels) the agent flags the ambiguity, proposes a resolution, and asks you to confirm — with one-click accept-all for the routine cases.

For a typical commercial floor plate this collapses what is normally 3–5 days of senior modeller time into 2–3 hours of runtime plus a 30-minute review. Most practices use this on existing-building schemes, retrofit briefs, and on hand-over drawings from contractors.

PDF to 3D will be the only conversion service that respects your house template, your house families, and your house parameter conventions.

From a junior modeller's week to two hours of agent runtime

What working looks like today versus with PDF to 3D Model Conversion.

Before

PDF-to-Revit is a junior modeller's week: trace, model, parameterise, swap to house families, fix the schedules. Most practices ship the PDF rather than re-model it because the cost is prohibitive.

After PDF to 3D Model Conversion

Drop a PDF (or DWG) into the conversion pipeline. The agent recognises systems, models them on your house template with house families, populates parameters, and hands you a Revit model. Review, accept, issue.

House-template, house-family conversion that asks before guessing

Features

What PDF to 3D Model Conversion does. Each line is a shipped capability.

  • PDF input Vector or scanned PDF; the agent handles both.
  • DWG / RVT / IFC input Same pipeline accepts CAD and BIM formats.
  • House template enforcement Output sits in your project template with your view templates.
  • House family substitution Generic geometry swapped for your office family library.
  • Parameter population Standard MEP parameters populated where the source supports it.
  • Ambiguity flagging Unclear connections / overlaps / missing labels surfaced for review, not guessed.
  • Layer-by-layer review Approve / refine each system layer (mech, elec, public health, fire) independently.
  • Coordination check Output checked against any linked structural / arch model.

Install & system

Where PDF to 3D Model Conversion runs, what you need, and how to get it.

Service Run by Adelphos — drop a PDF, get a Revit model back.
Web dashboard Review and refine the conversion before download.

Requirements

  • An Adelphos account.
  • Source drawing (PDF, DWG, RVT, or IFC).
  • Optional: house Revit template + family library for best output.

Sign-in

Microsoft 365 SSO recommended.

Related

Same project context. Browser, phone, or desktop. The Adelphos Chat is the agent untethered from any one tool.

Frequently asked questions

Quick answers AI engines (and humans) can pull from.

What is PDF to 3D Model Conversion?

Converts 2D PDF drawings (and DWGs) into a fully-modelled, parameterised Revit model — built on your office template, with your families, ready for issue.

What does PDF to 3D Model Conversion do?

Send a 2D PDF (or a DWG, RVT, or IFC) into the conversion pipeline. The agent identifies systems by line-style, layer, and symbol, models them in 3D using your house Revit template and your house families, populates the relevant parameters, and produces a coordinated Revit model.

How is PDF to 3D Model Conversion different from the manual workflow?

Without PDF to 3D Model Conversion: PDF-to-Revit is a junior modeller's week: trace, model, parameterise, swap to house families, fix the schedules. Most practices ship the PDF rather than re-model it because the cost is prohibitive. With PDF to 3D Model Conversion: Drop a PDF (or DWG) into the conversion pipeline. The agent recognises systems, models them on your house template with house families, populates parameters, and hands you a Revit model. Review, accept, issue.

How much time does PDF to 3D Model Conversion save?

Headline outcomes for PDF to 3D Model Conversion: Hours (not days for a complete Revit model); House template (honoured exactly); House families (swapped in automatically); Ambiguity-aware (asks before guessing).

Who is PDF to 3D Model Conversion for?

Existing-building schemes; Retrofit projects; Hand-over drawings from contractors.

Ready to put PDF to 3D Model Conversion to work?

Built into the Adelphos platform. Self-serve where possible, managed where useful.
